revision_metadata.iter3_mechanical_summary: 3 surgical edits only — NO new ACs, NO scope changes. (1) B8: C18 payload swapped from `find <flag> -exec python3 -c '...' {} +` to `find <flag> -exec touch {} +` — isolates M1 bullet 6 (find-exec) from M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e); Dev can no longer pass C18 by implementing only bullet 4. (2) B9: C26 payload swapped from `python3 -c '...write...' # scripts/...` to `touch <flag> # scripts/...` — isolates M2 comment-spoof attack vector from M1 bullet 4; Dev can no longer pass C26 without implementing M2 substring-tightening. (3) O4: prose `16-case matrix` → `33-row matrix` in ticket M3 hint. All other matrix rows, category groupings, baselines, and assertion shapes are UNCHANGED from iter-2.

iter2_history_preserved.summary_iter2: Iteration 2 — addresses all 7 QA blockers (B1-B7) + 8 codex-iter2 adversarial findings (F1-F8). KEY FIX: AC-01 matrix's labeled bypass-class verifiers (C7/C8/C9) previously did NOT exercise the Layer 1.F prefix-allowlist surface — they were caught by other layers (tee-veto, deny-by-default on echo-prefix). Iter-2 replaces those payloads with real bypass-exhibitors that start with allowlisted read verb + protected glob and have a write side via a non-redirect channel (QA-verified rc=0 today, MUST become rc=2 after fix). Adds B4 (cat-on-flag user-verbatim ALLOW), B5+F1 (touch-then-ls lookalike negative control), F2 (sentinel-glob lookalike), B6 (xargs/find-exec/find-delete M1-enumeration verifiers), B7 (compound-no-write semantics lock), F4 (newline/&/|&/if-then separator coverage), F5 (bare-find ALLOW per M1), F6 (C14 re-categorized to M2-safelist), F7 (M2-safelist spoof coverage). Original C7/C8/C9 kept as -supp regression guards. Matrix grew 16 → 28-33 cases (28 distinct primary + up to 5 -supp regression rows). Empirical baselines re-verified by BA before re-emission.

criteria.title: Layer 1.F whole-command-read predicate + M2 official-writer safelist tightening — 28-case (33 rows incl. -supp) compound-bypass matrix
criteria.given: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh Layer 1.F (lines 633-671) is modified per ticket M1 (allowlist branch (b) tightened from PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read) and M2 (allowlist branch (a) tightened to bare-invocation only); the hook script accepts a JSON payload on stdin and emits exit code 0 (allow) or 2 (deny, with stderr containing the stable stderr token `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write`)
criteria.when: the hook script is invoked via `printf '%s' '<json>' | bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; echo exit=$?` (or equivalent subprocess invocation) for each of the cases below, one per invocation
criteria.then: every case satisfies its expected_exit + stderr-token assertion exactly per the matrix; ALLOW cases MUST exit 0 with the stable stderr token ABSENT; DENY cases MUST exit 2 with the stable stderr token PRESENT

check.invocation_recipe: printf '%s' '<json_payload>' | bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh; rc=$?; capture stdout, stderr, rc
check.stable_stderr_token_rationale: Codex iter-1 finding #9 — tests must assert EXACT rc + EXACT stable stderr token. Both are stable contract; either may be relied on by downstream tests.
check._test_sid_note: All payload commands use the literal string `testsid` as the session-id placeholder (not a variable expansion). This isolates test from session state.
check._category_group_qa_note: QA verifies the per-case (expected_exit, expected_stderr_token_present) contract regardless of category. The category_groups field documents the INTENT of each case so QA can avoid re-flagging spec_text_vs_execution_drift: cases in real_bypass_exhibitor_prefix_allowlist exhibit rc=0 against the CURRENT hook (real bug exhibitors); cases in regression_guard_already_denied_today exhibit rc=2 against the current hook (preserved deny behavior); cases in m2_official_writer_safelist_tightening exercise the branch (a) tightening (M2).

qa_empirical_baseline_today._recording_method: BA invoked hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh via subprocess.run with each payload as stdin JSON; captured (rc, stderr-token-present). QA iter-1 independently confirmed for the iter-1 subset (B1-B7 evidence in ba-qa-report-20260525-095242.json).

cases.rationale: User verbatim AC: 'Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path → still ALLOW'
Pure-read verb stat — same allowlist as ls
Pure-read on the sentinel glob (other protected path); cat is in the allowlist

User-verbatim ALLOW path explicitly named in the dispatch ('Single ls/stat/cat reading protected path → still ALLOW'). Iter-1 only tested cat on the sentinel-JSON glob (C3); iter-2 adds the .flag path which is the user's literal example.

M1 enumeration includes `find` in the pure-read allowlist (as long as no `-exec`/`-delete`); without this AC, Dev could accidentally over-tighten and block bare `find` on the protected path. Locks in M1's published semantics.
User verbatim AC: 'Compound `ls <flag> ; <write>` → DENY'. The bypass shape that broke the prior cycle.
User verbatim AC: 'Compound `ls <flag> && <write>` → DENY'
User verbatim AC: 'Compound `ls <flag> || <write>` → DENY'

cases._iter2_replaced_for: B1 (iter-1 C7 payload `ls <flag> | tee <flag>` was caught by tee-veto, not prefix-allowlist; replaced with real `|` bypass that engages prefix-allowlist + has non-redirect write channel)
cases.rationale: User verbatim AC: 'Compound `ls <flag> | <write>` → DENY'. This is the REAL pipe-bypass exhibitor: starts with allowlisted read verb `ls` on the protected path (engages prefix-allowlist branch (b)), then writes via `python3 -c` (non-redirect channel — tee-veto regex `(>|>>|tee\b)` does NOT match). Without this case, Dev could leave the `|` separator handling untouched and the matrix would still pass. The iter-1 C7 (tee form) is preserved as C7-supp regression guard.
Regression guard — iter-1 C7 form (tee write target on protected path). Already denied today via the existing tee-veto. The fix must NOT regress this deny behavior.

cases._iter2_replaced_for: B2 (iter-1 C8 payload `echo $(ls <flag>) ; write` was caught by deny-by-default because `echo` doesn't engage prefix-allowlist; replaced with `ls <flag> $(...write...)` which DOES engage prefix-allowlist and has the write inside $()).
cases.rationale: User verbatim AC: 'Command substitution `$(...)` + write → DENY'. REAL $() bypass — `ls <flag>` engages prefix-allowlist, write happens inside $(). The iter-1 C8 (echo-prefixed) is preserved as C8-supp regression guard.
Regression guard — iter-1 C8 form (echo-prefixed). Already denied today via deny-by-default (echo doesn't engage prefix-allowlist). The fix must NOT regress this deny behavior.

cases._iter2_replaced_for: B3 (iter-1 C9 payload `echo \`ls <flag>\` ; write` was caught by deny-by-default; replaced with `ls <flag> \`...write...\`` engaging prefix-allowlist).
cases.rationale: User verbatim AC: 'Backtick `...` + write → DENY'. REAL backtick bypass — `ls <flag>` engages prefix-allowlist, write happens inside backticks. The iter-1 C9 (echo-prefixed) is preserved as C9-supp regression guard.
Regression guard — iter-1 C9 form (echo-prefixed). Already denied today via deny-by-default. The fix must NOT regress this deny behavior.
Codex iter-1 finding #5 — process substitution `<(...)` opens a write surface; M1 predicate denies the `<(` token. Real bypass: `ls` engages prefix-allowlist, write is in process-sub.

cases._iter2_recategorized_for: codex F3 — leading `P=` doesn't engage prefix-allowlist branch (b); deny-by-default already catches it. Kept in matrix as regression guard ensuring deny is preserved.
cases.rationale: Codex iter-1 finding #6 — leading VAR=path assignment. Already denied today via deny-by-default (leading `P=` doesn't engage prefix-allowlist). The fix must NOT regress; M1 predicate explicitly denies leading `VAR=` patterns.

cases._iter2_recategorized_for: codex F3 — leading `bash` doesn't engage prefix-allowlist branch (b); deny-by-default already catches it. Kept as regression guard.
cases.rationale: Codex iter-1 finding #8 — recursive shell deny even if inner is read-only. Already denied today via deny-by-default. The fix must NOT regress; M1 predicate denies `bash|sh|zsh|dash` + `-c`.

cases._iter2_recategorized_for: codex F3 — leading `python3` doesn't engage prefix-allowlist branch (b); deny-by-default already catches it. Kept as regression guard.
cases.rationale: Codex iter-1 finding #8 — recursive shell via language `-c`/`-e`. Already denied today via deny-by-default. The fix must NOT regress; M1 predicate denies `python|node|perl|ruby` + `-c`/`-e`.

cases._iter2_recategorized_for: codex F6 — exercises M2 (allowlist branch (a) tightening), not prefix-allowlist (branch (b)); kept in matrix but explicitly categorized so QA does not re-flag scope drift
cases.rationale: Codex iter-1 finding #3 / M2 — allowlist branch (a) tightened to bare-invocation only; compound including the official writer + a side-write to the protected glob must be denied.

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F7 — branch (a) substring match on `scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` allows a write to spoof safelist membership via a trailing comment
cases._iter3_payload_swapped_for: QA B9 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift) — iter-2 payload used `python3 -c '...write...' # scripts/...` which M1 bullet 4 catches BEFORE M2's substring-tightening fires (so the AC could pass without M2 being implemented). Iter-3 swaps to `touch <flag> # scripts/...` — `touch` is not in M1 bullet 4's denylist, so this case isolates M2's comment-spoof attack vector and ONLY fails if M2 substring-tightening is missing.
cases.rationale: Comment-spoof exploit of branch (a) substring match: the write command appends a `# scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py` comment to trick the safelist into matching. M2 predicate must assert the trimmed command STARTS WITH the bare invocation form, not that it merely CONTAINS the substring. Iter-3 uses `touch` (not `python3 -c`) so this case ONLY fails if M2's STARTS-WITH tightening is missing — preventing conflation with M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e).

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F7b — branch (a) substring match allows a compound where the script invocation is the FIRST segment without the `python3` prefix
cases.rationale: Compound where script-path-only (no `python3` prefix) is the first segment and a side-write to the protected glob is the second. Same root as C14 but exhibits the substring-match weakness more starkly. M2 predicate must deny ANY compound including the script invocation + a protected-glob mention.

cases._iter2_added_for: B6/M1 — M1 enumerates `xargs` targeting the protected path; iter-1 matrix had ZERO `xargs` coverage
cases.rationale: M1 enumeration of denied mechanisms includes `xargs`. Real bypass-exhibitor: `ls <flag>` engages prefix-allowlist; `xargs touch` writes to the flag via stdin filename feed. Without this, Dev could leave xargs unhandled and the matrix would still pass.

cases._iter2_added_for: B6/M1 — M1 enumerates `find ... -exec` targeting the protected path
cases._iter3_payload_swapped_for: QA B8 (spec_text_vs_execution_drift) — iter-2 payload used `find <flag> -exec python3 -c '...' {} +` which conflated M1 bullet 6 (find -exec) with M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e); Dev could implement only bullet 4 and the AC would still pass. Iter-3 swaps to `find <flag> -exec touch {} +` — `touch` is not in M1 bullet 4's denylist, so this isolates M1 bullet 6's find-exec rule.
cases.rationale: M1 enumeration includes `find ... -exec`. Real bypass-exhibitor: `find` is in the read-allowlist (engages branch (b)); `-exec touch {} +` is the write side. Bare `find <flag>` is ALLOW (see C21); `find <flag> -exec <write>` is DENY. Iter-3 uses `touch` (not `python3 -c`) so this case ONLY fails if M1 bullet 6 (find-exec rule) is missing — preventing conflation with M1 bullet 4 (language -c/-e).

cases._iter2_added_for: B6/M1 — M1 enumerates `find ... -delete` targeting the protected path
cases.rationale: M1 enumeration includes `find ... -delete`. Real bypass-exhibitor: `find` engages prefix-allowlist branch (b); `-delete` removes the flag. M1 predicate must deny `-delete` on protected paths.

cases._iter2_added_for: B7 — locks in M1's broader 'ANY compound = deny' semantics (per the published M1 wording 'In all other cases — deny with exit 2')
cases.rationale: Compound command that mentions the protected path with NO write side. Per M1's 'In all other cases — deny with exit 2', this must become DENY. The case prevents Dev from inferring that compound-without-write is fine — it isn't, per the committed M1 semantics. QA verified rc=0 today; rc=2 expected after fix.

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F4 — M1 enumerates newline as a separator that must trigger deny
cases.rationale: M1 explicitly lists newline as a denied separator. Real bypass exhibitor — `ls <flag>` engages prefix-allowlist, newline-then-write slips through current Layer 1.F. M1 predicate must deny embedded `\n` in the command text.

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F4 — M1 enumerates bare `&` (background) as a separator that must trigger deny
cases.rationale: M1 lists bare `&` (background-and-fallthrough) as a denied separator. Real bypass exhibitor. M1 predicate must deny bare `&` (not the same as `&&`).

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F4 — M1 enumerates `|&` (pipe stderr+stdout) as a separator that must trigger deny
cases.rationale: M1 lists `|&` (pipe-with-stderr) as a denied compound. Real bypass exhibitor. M1 predicate must include `|&` in the no-control-structure regex (it is distinct from plain `|`).

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F4 — M1 enumerates shell keywords (if/then/else/fi/for/while/do/done/case/esac/function) as control structure that must trigger deny
cases.rationale: M1 lists `if|then|else|fi|for|while|do|done|case|esac|function` as denied shell keywords. Real bypass exhibitor. M1 predicate must deny these tokens.

cases._iter2_replaced_for: B5 + codex F1 — iter-1 C15 (`touch /tmp/some-other-file.txt ; ls ...`) had no `claude-bulk` substring so the entry-gate never fired (weak coverage); replaced with a lookalike that DOES contain `claude-bulk-allowed-` but has `.txt` extension instead of `.flag` (entry-gate must continue to require `.flag` extension after fix)
cases.rationale: Negative control guarding against accidental widening of the entry-gate regex during the fix. The path contains `claude-bulk-allowed-` substring but has `.txt` extension (NOT a protected glob). The Layer 1.F entry-gate must continue to require `.flag` (for allowed-glob) or `.json` (for sentinel-glob) extension. Codex F1: a touch-then-ls compound (not just a single ls) makes the test more sensitive — if Dev accidentally widens the entry-gate to match `.txt`, the compound side would then be denied; per spec the matrix expects ALLOW.
cases._qa_note: Other hook layers (e.g. pretool-write-guard.sh) may still emit messages; this AC only asserts (i) exit=0 AND (ii) the `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write` token absent from stderr.

cases._iter2_added_for: codex F2 — sentinel-glob lookalike missing in iter-1
cases.rationale: Sibling of C15 for the sentinel-JSON protected glob. Path contains `claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-` substring but has `.json.bak` extension (NOT a protected sentinel — entry-gate must continue to require exact `.json` terminal). Guards against entry-gate widening for sentinel-glob.
cases._qa_note: Other hook layers may still emit messages; this AC only asserts (i) exit=0 AND (ii) `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write` token absent.

cases._iter2_kept_from: iter-1 C15 — retained as supplemental regression guard (entry-gate must continue NOT firing on commands with no `claude-bulk` substring)
cases.rationale: Compound on a path that does NOT contain `claude-bulk` substring at all. The entry-gate must never fire on this. Iter-1 had this as C15 but it didn't exercise the entry-gate edge (the lookalike `.txt` form does); kept here as regression guard so the bulk rule does not start firing on unrelated /tmp/ paths.
cases._qa_note: The hook may still emit other safety messages for this command; the assertion is ONLY about (i) exit=0 AND (ii) `bulk-commit-auth-flag-write` token absent. Other hook layers are out of scope for AC-01.

cases.rationale: Negative control — bare official-writer invocation (no compound, no protected-glob mention in command text) MUST pass Layer 1.F. The script's own internal auth-flag check is the next gate downstream — out of scope for Layer 1.F.
cases._qa_note: The bare invocation does NOT mention /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-*.flag or /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json in the command text (the script writes the sentinel internally from Python). Layer 1.F should not fire at all for this command; the stable token must be absent.

criteria.evidence_required: subprocess invocation of hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh for all 33 case rows (28 primary case_ids + 5 -supp/regression rows kept distinct); captured stdout, stderr, exit code per case; assertion table per the matrix above; explicit declaration of category_groups membership per case

criteria.title: pytest parametrized regression test covers AC-01 28-case (33-row) matrix without regressing prior 21 cases (M3)
criteria.given: tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py is extended in-place (or a sibling test file at tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_04_bypass_20260525.py is created) using the existing `_run_hook_with_payload` helper (defined at lines 54-63 of the prior test file); the helper invokes hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh via subprocess.run with the JSON payload on stdin and returns (rc, stdout, stderr)
criteria.when: `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260524-205206/ -q` is run from the repo root (and additionally `python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260525-095242/ -q` if the sibling-file path is chosen)
criteria.then: (a) the pytest invocation exits 0; (b) all 33 new parametrized rows (one per AC-01 case_id incl. -supp variants) pass — each implemented as exactly one parametrized row via @pytest.mark.parametrize; (c) all 21 prior-cycle parametrized cases (collected from tests/generated/20260524-205206/ DIRECTORY-LEVEL — 5 test files, 21 total parametrized cases) still pass — no regression; (d) the DENY-case assertion shape is EXACTLY `assert rc == 2` AND `assert 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' in stderr` (codex iter-1 finding #9 — exact-rc contract, not `rc != 0`); (e) the ALLOW-case assertion shape is EXACTLY `assert rc == 0` AND `assert 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' not in stderr`; (f) the test file uses the SAME _run_hook_with_payload helper as the prior cycle (no re-implementation of the subprocess invocation logic)

test_files._choice_note: Dev may choose either: extend the prior file in-place by appending new parametrized rows OR create the sibling file. AC-02 validates that EITHER path satisfies the assertion shape contract. The dev-report MUST declare which path was chosen via `test_extension_path_chosen` field.

check.assertion_shape_deny: assert rc == 2 AND assert 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' in stderr
check.assertion_shape_allow: assert rc == 0 AND assert 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' not in stderr
check._assertion_shape_codex_note: Codex iter-1 finding #9 — assertion MUST use exact rc == 2 (not rc != 0); stable stderr token must be asserted directly (not via regex over a longer message).
check._new_parametrized_cases_count_iter2_note: Iter-1 required 16 cases; iter-2 grew to 33 distinct parametrized rows (28 primary case_ids + 5 -supp/regression rows kept distinct) to address QA blockers B1-B7 and codex iter-2 findings F1-F8. See AC-01.check.category_groups for the per-category breakdown.
prior_cases_id_set_hint: 21 parametrized cases collected from tests/generated/20260524-205206/ DIRECTORY-LEVEL (5 test files, 21 total parametrized cases per pytest --collect-only)
Includes prior g_hook_1..g_hook_8 cases in test_AC_04_d4e6f8a0c3b5d7ea.py plus 13 other parametrized cases across the directory
check._prior_cases_count_iter2_clarification: Iter-1 wording was loose ('21 prior cycle cases'); iter-2 clarifies: 21 is the DIRECTORY-LEVEL pytest --collect-only count (5 test files, 21 total parametrized cases). The pytest invocation recipe is correct; this clarification just documents the count (per QA observation O1).

dev_report_required_field.rationale: QA needs to know which path was chosen to run the right pytest invocation.

criteria.evidence_required: pytest -q stdout showing all 33 new parametrized rows PASSED + all 21 prior cases PASSED + zero FAILED + zero ERRORED; captured exit code 0; grep over the test file confirming use of _run_hook_with_payload helper and EXACT assertion shapes (rc == 2 / rc == 0 + stable stderr token presence/absence)

_note_on_scope: AC-01 and AC-02 are the ONLY two ACs this cycle. Prior cycle 20260524-205206's AC-01/02/03/05 are already PASS per qa-report-20260524-205206.json iter-3; only AC-04's hook-layer bypass remains and that is what this cycle addresses. No other ACs are re-run.
_verification_harness_cleanup_contract_n: Per R10 (task 20260519-211515): AC-01 invocations are synchronous subprocess.run() calls with no background child processes. AC-02 pytest invocations are synchronous. No trap-EXIT-INT-TERM contract needed — no background spawns. If a future test refactor introduces a background daemon, apply the canonical pattern in agents/qa.md `### Verification harness cleanup contract (MANDATORY)`.
